title: Express Middleware, Promise Errors, Dependencies, and Response Ownership · Inference Brief: Express5 Promise Errors
summary: Middleware runs in an unsafe order, async rejections are wrapped twice, error handlers use the wrong signature, dependencies are created per request, and multiple layers write responses. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts express5 promise errors increment in composed middleware, dependency, and error boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use Express 5 automatic Promise rejection forwarding while preserving causal error identity and avoiding duplicate next calls.
Compose request identity, trust, limits, parsing, authentication, authorization, application, error, and observation middleware in a defended order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Express5 Promise Errors · Theory Express5 Promise Errors
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 2243 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to trace defended middleware order while keeping error and response ownership explicit; preserve response ownership after a client abort; then reuse an idempotency key with different input and reject the fingerprint mismatch. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use Express 5 automatic Promise rejection forwarding while preserving causal error identity and avoiding duplicate next calls.
